title: Temple of the Ancients
content:

}}
{{Quote|There is little I can tell you about the Temple. I know the secrets of our ancestors are contained within.|An [[Elders (Rakatan)|Elder]] councilor, to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
The '''Temple of the Ancients''', also called the '''Temple of the Elders''', was a [[Rakatan temple]] on [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]] that contained a [[Rakata Computer|computer]] that provided information about the [[Star Forge]]. The temple's doors were blocked by a [[Force field/Legends|force field]] that only [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] could bring down by performing a special ritual or by using an [[Unidentified tome|ancient tome]], and even then, the force field would not stay down for long.

==Description==
{{Quote|Only those who can command the Force can enter. Long ago we Rakata had this power.|An Elder councilor, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
The structure itself was twenty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Located on the top of the Temple of the Ancients was the summit. It contained an elevator to the main floor, as well as a [[disruptor field]] that forced [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to land, either controlled or not, on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

A [[Rakata Computer|computer]] containing extensive information about the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the history of their [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]], and the [[Star Forge]], was located in the Temple Catacombs. In [[3956 BBY]], when [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] visited the Temple for the second time, the computer was still functioning,<ref name="KOTOR" /> but in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], when [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] visited the Temple, the computer was broken beyond repair and could no longer be used by anyone.<ref name="PoD">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''</ref>

==History==
===Jedi Civil War===
{{Quote|During the collapse of the Rakata Empire, the warlords unleashed weapons of terrible destruction that threatened to drive our species to extinction. In the devastation's aftermath our ancestors locked the knowledge of such technology away in the Temple so it could never be used again.|An Elder councilor, to Revan|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
[[File:RakataTemple.png|thumb|left|300px|Rakata outside the Temple.]]
When several [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] were stranded on [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]], they built a camp at the Temple of the Ancients until they were killed by Revan.<ref name="KOTOR" />

During the [[Jedi Civil War]], Revan and crewmembers [[Zaalbar]], [[Mission Vao]], [[Carth Onasi]], [[Jolee Bindo]], [[Juhani]], [[HK-47]], [[T3-M4]], and [[Canderous Ordo]] crash-landed on Lehon while on their way to infiltrate the Star Forge. Their crash-landing was caused by the disruptor field installed within the temple. With their ship damaged and in need of repair, Revan set out with his companions to scout out the area for possible replacement parts from the other crashed ships which littered the area and to find a way to shut down the disruptor field.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Revan met with [[The One]], leader of the [[Black Rakata]], who made him a deal. The One would help him enter the Temple of the Ancients if Revan brought back an [[Unidentified tome|ancient tome]] from the [[Elders (Rakatan)|Elders]], another tribe living on Lehon. Revan, however, sided with the Elders. When Revan helped the Elders free a prisoner from the Black Rakata, the Elders performed a ritual allowing Revan into the temple.<ref name="KOTOR" />

The Elders had only started their ritual when Juhani and Bindo rushed up to them and told them they had sensed great danger in the temple, and they couldn't let Revan face it alone. At first the Elders refused, but after Revan insisted that he could not destroy the Star Forge alone, the Elders agreed to let all three Jedi inside.<ref name="KOTOR" />

In addition to [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] and a number of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] who had been sent to the temple by [[Darth Malak]], they discovered an ancient [[Rakata Computer|computer terminal]] which taught them about the history of the Rakata, the Infinite Empire, and the Star Forge and told them to go to the summit of the temple to disable the disruptor field. When Revan, Jolee, and Juhani arrived at the summit of the temple, they encountered [[Bastila Shan]], now Malak's new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. After a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Duel on the Temple of the Ancients|duel]], Bastila tried to convince Revan to reclaim his title as the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], but Revan refused. Bastila fled, and Revan deactivated the disruptor field generated from the summit before proceeding to his [[Battle of Rakata Prime|final confrontation]] with Malak. Shortly after, a celebration was held at the Temple Exterior, where Revan and his companions were personally awarded the [[Cross of Glory]] by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Forn Dodonna]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

===After the Jedi Civil War===
[[File:TempleoftheAncients.png|thumb|right|300px|The Temple around the time of the Galactic War.]]
Three centuries later, during the [[Galactic War]], the Temple was occupied by the [[Order of Revan]] and repurposed as a factory to create invincible cyborg supersoldiers equipped with Rakata technology for the Order's [[Infinite Army]]. When a strike team invaded, the cyborgs were destroyed and the supervising Revanites, [[Darth Arkous]] and [[Rian Darok]], were killed rather than be captured. Subsequently, Revan arrived in a [[Harrower-class dreadnought|''Harrower''-class dreadnought]] and opened fire on the Temple in an attempt to kill his enemies but the strike team escaped via shuttle.<ref name="Legacy of the Rakata" />

In [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], Darth Bane would arrive at the Temple to seek its secrets. In the lowest sublevel, he finally found [[Darth Revan's Sith Holocron|Darth Revan's holocron]]. Bane studied from it extensively until it lost power and died. The holocron [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper's]] words would inspire Bane to establish the [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]]. Inside the Temple, he was confronted by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Kas'im]]. Bane and Kas'im engaged in a [[Duel on Lehon|protracted duel]] that eventually moved to the steps of the Temple. Bane was then able to kill Kas'im by striking him with a powerful wave of Force energy that brought down the Temple entrance way around him. Bane then departed, and the further history of the Temple is unknown.<ref name="PoD" />

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]],'' the player is given the choice of killing the Elders as he was told by the One instead of siding with them. Doing this allows the player to recover an ancient tome, which Revan could then present to the One, and the Black Rakata then disable the shields.

Later, inside the temple itself, if Revan chooses to follow the dark side, he reclaims his title as [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Revan, and kills companions Jolee and Juhani. Revan and Bastila return to the ''Ebon Hawk'' and tell the crew of Revan's return to the dark side.

Also, the Elders say that only people who are strong in the Force can enter the Temple. But if the player chooses non-Jedi characters from his group after disabling the disruptor field and exiting the Temple, they will also be able to enter.
